title: Building Fast Websites with Qwik
excerpt: Why we chose Qwik for our marketing site and how resumability improves performance.
body:
Modern frameworks often ship too much JavaScript. Qwik takes a different approach: resumability. The server sends HTML and only the minimal JS needed for interactivity is loaded when the user needs it.

For a marketing site, that means faster First Contentful Paint and Time to Interactive, especially on mobile. We use Qwik City for routing and server-side rendering, and Tailwind for styling.

If you're considering a new frontend, give Qwik a look for content-heavy or marketing sites where every kilobyte counts.

title: API Design Best Practices for Mobile and Web
excerpt: REST and versioning, auth, and how to keep your API maintainable as you scale.
body:
A well-designed API makes both web and mobile clients easier to build and maintain. We recommend REST with clear resource naming, consistent status codes, and versioning in the URL path.

Use JSON for request and response bodies, and document everything with OpenAPI or similar. For auth, prefer tokens (e.g. Laravel Sanctum) with short expiry and refresh flows.

Finally, add rate limiting and CORS from day one so you don't have to retrofit later.

title: Why We Use Laravel for Backends
excerpt: Laravel remains our go-to for APIs and admin tools. Here's why.
body:
Laravel gives us a solid foundation: routing, validation, Eloquent ORM, queues, and a strong ecosystem. For agency work, that means we spend less time on boilerplate and more on business logic.

Laravel Sanctum makes SPA and mobile auth straightforward. When we need to ship an API plus an admin dashboard, Laravel lets us do both in one codebase with clear separation.

We still pick Node or Go when the problem fits better, but for most CRUD-heavy products, Laravel is our default.
